children.heading: Exemptions from State laws, rules, regulations, or orders
children.label: a Exemptions from State laws, rules, regulations, or orders

children.text: prohibit the establishment of a purchasing group;

make it unlawful for an insurer to provide or offer to provide insurance on a basis providing, to a purchasing group or its members, advantages, based on their loss and expense experience, not afforded to other persons with respect to rates, policy forms, coverages, or other matters;
prohibit a purchasing group or its members from purchasing insurance on the group basis described in paragraph (2) of this subsection;

prohibit a purchasing group from obtaining insurance on a group basis because the group has not been in existence for a minimum period of time or because any member has not belonged to the group for a minimum period of time;
require that a purchasing group must have a minimum number of members, common ownership or affiliation, or a certain legal form;
require that a certain percentage of a purchasing group must obtain insurance on a group basis;
require that any insurance policy issued to a purchasing group or any members of the group be countersigned by an insurance agent or broker residing in that State; or
otherwise discriminate against a purchasing group or any of its members.

children.label: A
children.text: a purchasing group; or

children.label: B
children.text: any person who is a member of a purchasing group; and

children.label: A
children.text: liability coverage;

children.label: B
children.text: insurance related services; or

children.label: C
children.text: management services;

children.heading: Licensing of agents or brokers for purchasing groups
children.label: c Licensing of agents or brokers for purchasing groups
children.text: A State may require that a person acting, or offering to act, as an agent or broker for a purchasing group obtain a license from that State, except that a State may not impose any qualification or requirement which discriminates against a nonresident agent or broker.

children.heading: Notice to State insurance commissioners of intent to do business
children.label: d Notice to State insurance commissioners of intent to do business
A
children.text: shall identify the State in which such group is domiciled;

children.label: B
children.text: shall specify the lines and classifications of liability insurance which the purchasing group intends to purchase;

children.label: C
children.text: shall identify the insurance company from which the group intends to purchase insurance and the domicile of such company; and

children.label: D
children.text: shall identify the principal place of business of the group.
Such purchasing group shall notify the commissioner of any such State as to any subsequent changes in any of the items provided in such notice.

children.heading: Designation of agent for service of documents and process
children.label: e Designation of agent for service of documents and process

children.text: was domiciled before ; andApril 1, 1986

children.text: before , purchased insurance from an insurance carrier licensed in any State; andSeptember 25, 1981

which was a purchasing group under the requirements of this chapter before ; andOctober 27, 1986
as long as such group does not purchase insurance that was not authorized for purposes of an exemption under this chapter as in effect before .October 27, 1986

children.heading: Purchases of insurance through licensed agents or brokers acting pursuant to surplus lines laws
children.label: f Purchases of insurance through licensed agents or brokers acting pursuant to surplus lines laws
children.text: A purchasing group may not purchase insurance from a risk retention group that is not chartered in a State or from an insurer not admitted in the State in which the purchasing group is located, unless the purchase is effected through a licensed agent or broker acting pursuant to the surplus lines laws and regulations of such State.

children.heading: State powers to enforce State laws
children.label: g State powers to enforce State laws
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to affect the authority of any State to make use of any of its powers to enforce the laws of such State with respect to which a purchasing group is not exempt under this chapter.

children.heading: States’ authority to sue
children.label: h States’ authority to sue
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall affect the authority of any State to bring an action in any Federal or State court.
